level_signals: Comp range $365K-$435K base, consistent with senior IC at Anthropic
Expected to influence senior technical staff
Owns cross-functional programs with real-time allocation and trade-off authority
Background requirement: ML engineering/research before transitioning to program management

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have a background in ML engineering, ML research, or STEM R&D before transitioning to technical program management
Have deep, hands-on experience with ML training pipelines, RLHF systems, and large-scale data infrastructure in production.
Have a track record of building execution plans and inventing high-leverage processes that reduce operational overhead and let researchers focus on research.
Are a fast learner who builds deep contextual understanding in unfamiliar technical domains and can contribute meaningfully to discussions with researchers.
Are resourceful, high-agency, and able to navigate ambiguity and shifting priorities to drive progress in a fast-moving research setting
Have excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to influence senior technical staff through clarity, competence, and consistent delivery.

You'll need real technical depth: the ability to debug data pipelines, read RL transcripts to spot issues, and make allocation and quality decisions in real time when experimental or production runs hit problems.
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Are excited about the potential for AI to accelerate scientific discovery

scope.surface: Discovery team — AI scientist systems including compute planning, scientific RL environments, and vendor pipelines; potential scope into bio R&D incubation
scope.business_model: research-driven AI lab, frontier model development
primary_metrics_inferred: RL environment health (quality, reward integrity, failure rates)
cross_functional_partners: Life sciences and STEM research teams
core_responsibilities: Manage Discovery's compute planning across SL, RL, and sandboxing workloads, including forecasting, allocation, prioritization, and efficiency improvements
Monitor the health of scientific RL environments (quality, reward integrity, failure rates) and drive issues to resolution
Expedite the external vendor pipeline for RL environments, including quality review, reward design, and production integration
Work with research teams across life sciences, STEM, and other scientific domains to translate research goals into roadmaps that advance AI scientist capabilities
Establish processes and frameworks that bring structure to an unstructured research setting without slowing researchers down
Collaborate with research leads, infrastructure engineers, and data operations to identify blockers, prioritize competing needs, and make technical trade-off decisions

compensation.equity_form: optional equity donation matching mentioned (suggests options/RSUs are part of package)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($365K-$435K base)
Clear and specific program scope — compute planning, RL environment health, vendor pipelines
Role has genuine technical depth requirements, not a generic TPM posting
Encourages applications even from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Mission-driven team with explicit north star (AI scientist)
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Extremely high technical bar (ML engineering/research background required) combined with TPM scope may limit candidate pool significantly
Years of experience not specified, making it hard to gauge seniority expectations beyond compensation signal
Compensation range ($365K-$435K) is very wide ($70K spread), suggesting significant internal level ambiguity

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from compensation range and scope of influence; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and team context)
location.cities_listed (inferred from HQ reference; JD says 'one of our offices' but San Francisco is the only city named)
ambiguities: The JD body says 'at least 25% of the time' in office but notes 'some roles may require more time in our offices' — actual office requirement for this specific role is unclear and may exceed 25%.
Years of experience is not explicitly stated; the JD notes 'Years of experience required will correlate with the internal job level requirements for the position' without specifying the level.

The 'You May Be a Good Fit If You' framing is somewhat ambiguous — these read as strong requirements given the technical specificity, but the language is softer than 'Required'. All items treated as hard must-haves except the cultural/excitement item, which is soft.
